    Lokesh Das

    Assistant Professor, School of Computing

    Bio!

    I am an Assistant Professor in the School of Computing at Wichita State University where I direct the AIMS Lab. I received my PhD in Computer Science from the University of Memphis, advised by Dr. Myounggyu Won. I obtained my MS in Computer Science from the University of Memphis in 2022 and my B.Sc. in Computer Science & Engineering from the University of Dhaka Bangladesh, respectively. Before joining the Ph.D. program, I worked as a software engineer at Samsung R&D Institute Bangladesh and as a Senior Officer in the Information and Communication Technology Division at Agrani Bank PLC. My research interests are Intelligent Transportation Systems, Connected and Autonomous Vehicles, V2X Communication, Reinforcement Learning, Computer Vision, Speech Emotion Recognition, Cyber Security, Internet of Things (IoT).

    Latest News

    [March 2025] - Our paper was accepted at the 21st International Wireless Communications & Mobile Computing Conference (IWCMC 2025) Paper.
    [January 2025] - Yassine Ibork joined our research group as a Ph.D. Student. Welcome!
    [August 2024] - I joined the School of Computing at Wichita State University as a tenure-track assistant professor.
    [May 2024] - I successfully defended my Ph.D. dissertation titled A Machine Learning-Driven Approach for Next-Generation Traffic Control System for Autonomous Vehicles.
    [August 2023] - I presented my paper (as oral and poster presentations) at IROS’23.
    [August 2023] - I was awarded the College of Arts and Sciences Travel Enrichment Grant at the University of Memphis.
    [July 2023] - I successfully defended my Ph.D. proposal.
    [July 2023] - I received the Graduate Student Association Travel Funding at the University of Memphis.
    [June 2023] - Our paper was accepted at the International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC’23).
    [June 2023] - Our paper was accepted at the International Conference on Robots and Systems (IROS’23).
    [June 2023] - Our paper was accepted at the International Conference on Robots and Systems (IROS’23).
    [March 2023] - I presented my research at the IUCRC research meeting in Indianapolis.
    [May 2022] - I received my master’s in Computer Science from the University of Memphis.
    [May 2021] - Our paper was accepted at the International Conference on Machine Learning (ICML’21).
    [March 2021] - I presented my research at the 16th CS Research Symposium.
    [February 2021] - Our paper was accepted at the IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA’21).
    [May 2020] - Our project Tiger-Car-App was awarded the best project award at Software Engineering in Spring 2020.
    [August 2019] - I started my Ph.D. in Computer Science at the University of Memphis.
